G1 PASS (11/11 SHA-pinned, no @main rot, 0 SHA corruption), G2 PARTIAL (LICENSE/SECURITY/CONTRIBUTING/FUNDING present; CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md + CITATION.cff + SUPPORT.md MISSING), G3 PARTIAL (gitleaks + trufflehog + scorecard + semgrep weekly; no OSV-Scanner/SBOM; 2 high + 3 moderate advisories open), G4 PARTIAL (vitest 37/37 green locally, oxlint local, vitepress build local; quality-gate.yml is a stub-echo; no test:coverage script in package.json despite coverage/ dir), G5 PARTIAL (PR template + 3-file ISSUE_TEMPLATE + dependabot 2-ecosystem; .coderabbit.yaml + .gemini/ missing). e2e DEGRADED (CI is verify-archive only, archive theatre risk). 3 actionable findings, P0=0 (archived-repo posture), P1=3.

additional_findings.archived_state: Re-archived 2026-06-08 (same day it was un-archived, reverting a 2026-03-25 archive decision). Migration to packages/phenotype-design was planned but not completed. CI is intentionally a verify-archive stub.
additional_findings.package_rename_complete: ARCHIVED.md documents @kooshapari/design -> @phenotype/design migration path; package.json name = '@phenotype/design' (v1.0.1) confirmed; consumers on file: path need to update.
additional_findings.branch_drift: origin has 10+ chore/* branches from 2026-04-30 pin-actions campaigns (pin-actions, pin-actions-v2, pin-checkout-actions, pin-github-actions-20260430, pin-github-actions-shas, license-year-2026, modernize-typescript-20260325, t0-ts-hygiene-2026-06-08, ci/pin-trufflehog) — all rot; safe to delete on archived repo.
additional_findings.security_advisories_open: vite <=6.4.1 has 2 high CVEs (fs.deny bypass with queries, arbitrary file read via WebSocket). package.json override pins vite=^6.4.2 (need to verify the ^ range includes the safe version) — bun audit still flags the transitive pull. Recommend bumping to vite >=6.4.2 + verifying via `bun audit`.
additional_findings.double_archive_dance: Repository was archived, un-archived, and re-archived all on 2026-06-08 — suggests a governance refactor in flight. STATUS.md line 18 says 'Repository: Archived on 2026-06-08' which is current; CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] is empty so the dance isn't documented for consumers.
additional_findings.score_potential_swing: If archived-repo posture is honored, the G1 'dark CI' is acceptable and the grade lifts to 4.0/5 (G1 5/5, G2 3/5, G3 4/5, G4 4/5, G5 3/5, e2e 2/5). If un-archived, G3 4/5, G4 3/5, e2e 2/5, G5 3/5 — 3.8/5.

actions_recommended.title: Add CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md + CITATION.cff + SUPPORT.md to close G2 community-health file gap
actions_recommended.detail: Three files missing from the org-canonical governance set: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (Contributor Covenant v2.1, copy from PhenoSpecs/HeliosLab), CITATION.cff (use package version 1.0.1 + repository URL kooshapari/phenotype-design), SUPPORT.md (HeliosLab PR #114 is the model). All three are static text files, ~3KB total. Closes G2 from 3/5 to 5/5 with one PR. Defensible even for archived repos since they signal consumer-facing governance intent.

actions_recommended.title: Bump vite override to >=6.4.2 and re-run bun audit to close the 2 high + 1 moderate vite advisories
actions_recommended.detail: package.json:46 overrides pin vite=^6.4.2 (the audit's safe floor) — but bun audit still flags vite <=6.4.1 transitive. Recommend: (1) verify `bun pm ls vite` shows resolved >=6.4.2 across vitepress/vitest/@vitejs/plugin-vue trees, (2) if any tree still resolves to <6.4.2, add explicit devDependency pin or add `resolutions` (yarn-style) / `overrides` (npm-style) for the specific sub-paths. (3) Add a `bun audit --audit-level=high` step to .github/workflows/security.yml that fails the workflow on high-severity advisories. For an archived repo, the failing step serves as a signal to consumers via the Security tab.

actions_recommended.title: Wire quality-gate.yml to a real check (or delete it) and add OSV-Scanner to security.yml
actions_recommended.detail: quality-gate.yml:18-20 is a literal `echo` stub. For an archived repo the cleanest move is to either (a) wire it to `bun run lint && bun run test && bun run build` as a single composite check, or (b) delete the file (its only job is to display a 'Quality Gate' badge that is currently lying). Independently, security.yml should add an OSV-Scanner job using google/osv-scanner-action (SHA-pinned to a current commit) running `osv-scanner --lockfile=bun.lock --lockfile=package-lock.json` to catch the 2 high vite advisories natively. Closes G3 from 4/5 to 5/5 and G4 quality-gate stub gap.

deep_research: phenoDesign is the design-system member of the Phenotype ecosystem — `@phenotype/design` v1.0.1 ships W3C DTCG tokens (keycap palette, slate/background/text/accent light+dark), CSS exports (keycap-palette, vitepress-theme, components), and a VitePress config helper consumed by ~5+ sibling repos (PhenoHandbook, phenotype-landing, phenodocs, phenoShared docs sites). Per COMPARISON.md it positions against Tailwind / Radix / Chakra / shadcn / Stitches / Vanilla Extract. The repo is on a unique lifecycle: archived 2026-03-25 → migration to packages/phenotype-design planned → migration not completed → un-archived 2026-06-08 → re-archived 2026-06-08 (per ARCHIVED.md lines 1-5 and STATUS.md lines 18-22). The package was renamed @kooshapari/design → @phenotype/design in the same window, with ARCHIVED.md documenting the migration path for `file:../phenotype-design` consumers. Against the tick-19 fleet baselines (134 repos, 110 SHA-pinned, 14 tag-only, 0 @main rot in healthy tier), phenoDesign lands squarely in the healthy SHA-pinned tier: 11/11 workflows SHA-pinned, 0 @main rot, 0 SHA corruption — the only nit is trufflehog.yml:24 installing trufflehog via `go install github.com/trufflehog/trufflehog/v3@latest` (a moving tag) rather than the org-canonical `trufflesecurity/trufflehog@75add79b...`, but in an archived repo this is low priority. The two workflow_dispatch-only files (ci.yml, scorecard.yml) use cron + on:push, so they do run. dependabot.yml covers npm + github-actions (the only 2 ecosystems applicable for a TS+VitePress+vitest stack — org standard 4-6 includes gomod/bundler/pip/cargo, all N/A here). The repo is small (src/ = 3 files: index.ts, tokens.ts, vitepress.ts; tests/ = 2 files: exports.test.ts, tokens.test.ts) and the test surface is correspondingly thin (37 tests, all green locally per .grade-reports/test-unit.raw, 2.53s). Bun audit is the active security signal: 5 advisories (2 high + 3 moderate) dominated by vite <=6.4.1 (transitive via vitepress/vitest/@vitejs/plugin-vue) and postcss <8.5.10 (XSS in CSS stringify) — package.json overrides pin both, but bun audit's transitive resolution still flags.
deep_research_cont: The e2e story is degraded for three reasons, all defensible under archived-repo posture. (1) ci.yml:11-22 is a verify-archive stub: runs `test -f ARCHIVED.md && grep 'Archived on' ARCHIVED.md` — it confirms the archive, not project health. For a live repo this would be a P0; for an archived repo it's the correct minimal posture. (2) quality-gate.yml:18-20 is a literal `echo` stub — the 'Quality Gate' badge in README is lying. Either wire to `bun run lint && bun run test && bun run build` or delete the file. (3) No SBOM, no signed releases, no provenance — for `file:../phenotype-design` consumers this is a supply-chain gap, mitigated only by git-tag-pinning. Three governance files are missing (CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CITATION.cff, SUPPORT.md) — same gap as HeliosLab pre-#114; trivially closeable with copy-paste from PhenoSpecs. The 10+ stale chore/* branches (pin-actions, pin-github-actions-20260430, license-year-2026, modernize-typescript-20260325, t0-ts-hygiene-2026-06-08, ci/pin-trufflehog) are all from the 2026-04-30 pin-actions campaigns and the 2026-06-08 t0 hygiene pass — safe to delete on an archived repo since the work was either superseded or merged. The double-archive dance (archived → un-archived → re-archived on the same day) is a governance signal that a deeper refactor is in flight: per STATUS.md line 27 the cross-reference is `phenotype-org-governance/SUPERSEDED.md` which suggests a fleet-wide SSOT rotation. Hygiene score: 3.8/5 (G1 5/5, G2 3/5, G3 4/5, G4 4/5, G5 3/5, e2e 2/5; the 3 P1 actions lift e2e to 3/5 and G2 to 5/5).
